# terrible_amazon_aws_ec2_snapshot (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description to be applied to the snapshot.
- `device_name` (String) Device name of a mounted volume to be snapshotted.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `group_names` (String) The group to be added or removed. The possible value is `all`. Mutually exclusive with `user_ids`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `instance_id` (String) Instance that has the required volume to snapshot mounted.
- `last_snapshot_min_age` (Number) If the volume's most recent snapshot has started less than `last_snapshot_min_age` minutes ago, a new snapshot will not be created.
- `modify_create_vol_permission` (Boolean) If set to `true`, ec2 snapshot's createVolumePermissions can be modified.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `purge_create_vol_permission` (Boolean) Whether unspecified group names or user IDs should be removed from the snapshot createVolumePermission. Must set `modify_create_vol_permission` to `True` for when `purge_create_vol_permission` is set to `True`.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `snapshot_id` (String) Snapshot id to remove.
- `snapshot_tags` (String) A dictionary of tags to add to the snapshot. If the volume has a `Name` tag this will be automatically added to the snapshot.
- `state` (String) Whether to add or create a snapshot.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `user_ids` (String) The account user IDs to be added or removed. If createVolumePermission on snapshot is currently set to Public i.e. `group_names=all`, providing `user_ids` will not make createVolumePermission Private unless `modify_create_vol_permission` is set to `true`. Mutually exclusive with `group_names`.
- `volume_id` (String) Volume from which to take the snapshot.
- `wait` (Boolean) Wait for the snapshot to be ready.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `snapshots` (String) List of snapshots.
- `volume_size` (Number) The size of the volume, in GiB.
